name: Crab Fritters
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the first five ingredients in a medium bowl until well combined. Let the batter stand at room temperature for 1 hour.
2. In a large bowl, mix the crabmeat, bread crumbs, two egg whites, and parsley. Season with salt and pepper.
3. Divide the mixture into 24 mounds and press each mound firmly into a ball. Roll each crabmeat ball in flour; shake off excess.
4. Beat the remaining egg whites in a small bowl until stiff peaks form.
5. Fold the egg whites into the batter.
6. Heat oil in a deep fryer or heavy saucepan to 360°F (180°C).
7. Dip each crab ball, one at a time, into the batter, coating completely. Carefully lower into the oil.
8. Repeat with remaining crab balls, cooking until pale and golden brown, about 5 minutes. Use a slotted spoon to transfer fritters to paper towels and drain.
9. Serve with Curry Sauce. Do not overcrowd the fryer or saucepan when making these crab fritters. Make no more than 5 fritters at a time.
10. Ensure each fritter is gently slid into hot oil. Dropping them can cause dangerous splashes.
11. Turn the fritters frequently as they fry, ensuring all sides are browned.
12. For Curry Sauce: Heat olive oil in a small heavy skillet over medium heat. Add minced garlic and saute for 1 minute. Add curry powder and stir for 1 minute. Transfer to a blender, add remaining ingredients, and blend until smooth. Pour into a bowl.
